finding: The production deployment workflow used the redirect-continuity evaluator during served verification, but no dedicated contract required the adversarial redirect tests and workflow linkage to remain jointly executable in CI.
design_implication: A separate fail-closed GitHub Actions gate now runs the redirect-continuity adversarial suite and a workflow contract that verifies the production workflow passes Wrangler's returned deployment URL into the redirect-enforcing verifier.

verification.source_behavior: GitHub main branch returns the committed workflow with both exact Node test commands, push and pull-request path filters, read-only contents permission, and an explicit claim boundary.
verification.local_runtime_reason: The isolated execution environment could not resolve github.com, so a fresh-checkout Node execution was not possible.

privacy_boundary: Contains only public repository paths, commit identifiers, workflow structure, and non-secret blocker status.
claim_limits: Does not prove GitHub Actions completed successfully.
Does not prove Cloudflare credentials exist.
Does not prove deployment, DNS authority, provider ownership, or served commit identity.
falsification_route: Change either workflow so the exact adversarial test, contract test, returned deployment URL, or redirect-enforcing verifier linkage is removed; the contract gate should fail on its next execution.
